file_insights.changes: Updated module documentation and added comprehensive imports from base.py; exported 11 serializer classes and utility functions
file_insights.purpose: Package initialization with clean public API for serializer exports

file_insights.changes: New file with 585 lines of base serializer implementations
file_insights.purpose: Core serializer classes for DRF with standard response format handling

file_insights.changes: Expected viewset implementations (referenced but truncated in diff)
Expected base viewset implementations (referenced but truncated in diff)
file_insights.purpose: Base viewset classes with standard response format mixin

patterns_discovered.description: Serializers follow a hierarchical inheritance pattern: BaseModelSerializer → SoftDeleteModelSerializer → AuditModelSerializer → TenantModelSerializer
patterns_discovered.benefit: Progressive feature addition with composition, avoiding code duplication

patterns_discovered.description: TenantModelSerializer auto-injects organization from request context and validates cross-tenant references
patterns_discovered.benefit: Enforces security boundary automatically at serialization level, prevents accidental cross-tenant data exposure

patterns_discovered.description: Separate response wrapper serializers (StandardResponseSerializer, PaginatedResponseSerializer, ErrorResponseSerializer) for API documentation
patterns_discovered.benefit: Decouples data serialization from response envelope, enables consistent OpenAPI documentation

patterns_discovered.description: SoftDeleteModelSerializer excludes deleted_at from output and provides is_deleted computed field
patterns_discovered.benefit: Hides implementation details from API consumers while maintaining soft delete capability

patterns_discovered.description: created_at and updated_at fields are read-only with help_text
patterns_discovered.benefit: Prevents client manipulation of timestamps, improves API documentation

patterns_discovered.description: BaseModelSerializer.get_field_names() ensures 'id' field is always first
patterns_discovered.benefit: Consistent API response structure, improves client-side parsing predictability

gotchas_discovered.description: TenantModelSerializer requires request.organization to be set by middleware/auth; will silently use default if not present
gotchas_discovered.risk: HIGH - Cross-tenant data exposure if organization is not properly attached to request
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Must ensure authentication middleware properly populates request.organization before serializer validation

gotchas_discovered.description: deleted_at marked as write_only but not fully excluded; requires careful field filtering in Meta
gotchas_discovered.risk: MEDIUM - Could accidentally leak soft delete state if Meta.fields includes deleted_at
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Always use is_deleted computed field instead of direct deleted_at access in API responses

gotchas_discovered.description: TenantModelSerializer provides organization_id (read-only) but Meta still includes 'organization' as read_only
gotchas_discovered.risk: MEDIUM - Confusing API surface with duplicate organization information
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Document clearly which field to use; consider removing organization from fields entirely

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Response wrapper serializers not integrated with views
gotchas_discovered.description: StandardResponseSerializer, PaginatedResponseSerializer, ErrorResponseSerializer appear to be documentation-only
gotchas_discovered.risk: MEDIUM - If mixin is not properly implemented in views, response format may not match serializer definitions
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Verify that views/base.py properly implements the mixin to wrap responses according to these serializers

gotchas_discovered.description: TenantModelSerializer uses TYPE_CHECKING for Organization import but logic still references it at runtime
gotchas_discovered.risk: LOW - TYPE_CHECKING prevents circular imports but runtime validation may fail if Organization not available
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Ensure Organization model is properly importable; consider lazy imports in validation methods

approach_outcome.summary: Implemented comprehensive base serializer architecture with tenant isolation, audit fields, soft delete support, and standard response formatting
key_accomplishments: Created 4-level serializer inheritance hierarchy (Base → SoftDelete → Audit → Tenant)
Implemented automatic organization injection and cross-tenant validation
Added response wrapper serializers for API documentation
Defined utility serializers (IDListSerializer, BulkActionResponseSerializer, SlugLookupSerializer, MinimalSerializer)
Established clear field ordering convention with id-first pattern
Created comprehensive module documentation with usage examples and critical rules
approach_outcome.completeness: High - Core serializer classes fully implemented; response wrapper serializers in place; clear public API defined
quality_indicators: Type hints throughout (TYPE_CHECKING for circular imports)
Consistent meta class patterns across hierarchy
Help text on all fields for API documentation

recommendations.recommendation: Verify views/base.py properly implements response mixin and that ErrorResponseSerializer is used correctly for error cases
recommendations.rationale: Response format consistency is critical for API security; must ensure all endpoints follow the defined format

recommendations.recommendation: Ensure authentication middleware sets request.organization before any serializer validation occurs
recommendations.rationale: TenantModelSerializer depends on this context; missing context could allow cross-tenant data access

recommendations.recommendation: Clarify and document whether Meta.fields should include 'organization' when organization_id is already provided
recommendations.rationale: Reduces API surface confusion; prevents duplicate tenant identification in responses

recommendations.recommendation: Add examples to docstrings showing how to use each serializer with actual model classes
recommendations.rationale: Current examples are incomplete; developers need clear patterns for inheritance and Meta configuration

recommendations.recommendation: Create comprehensive test suite covering: tenant isolation, soft delete exclusion, organization auto-injection, cross-tenant detection
recommendations.rationale: These are security-critical features; need test coverage to prevent regressions

recommendations.recommendation: Consider extracting _validate_tenant_relations logic into a separate validator mixin for reusability
recommendations.rationale: Logic could be useful in form validators and other serializers; improves DRY principle
